title: Does the F1 twin rig (frozen vs adaptive codec, 10M, drill scale) have the power, instruments and a specified metric to make a null attributable to the mechanism? Verdict = rig LICENSED or REFUSED.
verdict: rig = REFUSED (2/5 PASS) — G-1/G-2 unevaluable (generator does not exist); G-4 triggered but its interpretation is a card defect (it tests the CONTROL arm, which is supposed to sit at chance); G-3/G-5 PASS. salvage l4(b) STANDS: M=109/120 p=1.9e-21, C3 liveness=110/120.
resolved: G-5 RESOLVED — the H_9288 protocol was intact in the v1 repo (`anima`), not lost. Recovery falsified two frozen premises: F2 is forced-choice bacc (chance 0.50), not an F-score; n_items=120.

title: Does the recovered anchor survive a correct bounds check, and can the generator produce the contrast the pilot needs?
verdict: rig = REFUSED (8/10) — ANCHOR HALF ALL GREEN (B-1..B-4: the correctly-specified bounds check confirms l4(b) stands, replicated). Generator half fails on 2: N-2 contrast (no K makes the frozen codec blind AND the refit codec atomic — affixes too rare in-stream for BPE to fuse) and N-4 cooccurrence (2363 — parse-ambiguous, not a real emission; line() emits 0 heldout+NEG).
